title: 1978-10-10_SECTION_016_356.txt
date_basis: Inherited from parent document folder date
key_claims: Dick asserts that his nature spans Acts and USA 1974 not through reincarnation, but because he possesses an immortal, non-episodic soul that encompasses vast time. "Thomas" and "PKD" are integrated parts of this single whole.
He fuses Dionysos of *The Bacchae*, Jesus, and YHWH into one rescuer entity that he experienced as the Void, VALIS, and "I am."

He concludes that his experiences confirm Platonism and Neo-Platonism: the fall into forgetfulness, the recovery of the *eide* (anamnesis), and Plotinus' ecstatic union with "The One" outside space and time.
Despite his Christian focus, he acknowledges that the mechanics of his cosmology are entirely Platonic. The particulars have only "derived reality," while the true world consists of the *eide*.
His ontological identity and fulfillment lie in being a revolutionary fighting against the Empire (the Black Iron Prison).
recurring_concepts: **Anamnesis**: The Platonist recovery of the universals/Forms.
**The Empire / BIP**: The oppressive worldly system that he, as a secret Christian revolutionary, is fighting.
reading_excerpt: Dick asserts that his nature spans Acts and USA 1974 not through reincarnation, but because he possesses an immortal, non-episodic soul that encompasses vast time. "Thomas" and "PKD" are integrated parts of this single whole.
